title: drosanthemum quadratum
intro: Drosanthemum quadratum, also known as the rock daisy or four-square drosanthemum, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its small, white or pale yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Drosanthemum quadratum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Drosanthemum quadratum can be prepared as an herbal remedy by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create a tea, tincture, or topical application for its medicinal uses.
